title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Resolve an integration conflict by reconstructing both intents and verifying behavior rather than deleting markers until Git accepts the file. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.
Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.
Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Conflict Stages · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

Which action demonstrates Conflict Stages without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Conflict Stages result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Git Reference — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

hints: Name the Conflict Stages precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Ours always means the feature branch and theirs always means main.

Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Conflict Stages evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Conflict Stages assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=2, and repair limit=3 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Conflict Stages has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Rerere Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=3, and repair limit=4 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record or reuse conflict resolutions only after verifying context, tests, and changed-case behavior.
